1.name: Easy
1.description: Factual questions directly from the text

2.name: Medium
2.description: Interpretive questions about meaning and themes

questions.question: What was the name of the man from Ramathaim-zophim?
questions.explanation: 1 Samuel 1:1 names the man Elkanah.

questions.question: What were the names of Elkanah’s two wives?
questions.explanation: His wives were Hannah and Peninnah.

questions.question: Which wife did Elkanah love and give a worthy portion to?
questions.explanation: He loved Hannah and gave her a worthy portion.

questions.question: Who provoked Hannah grievously?
questions.explanation: Peninnah provoked Hannah because the LORD had shut her womb.

questions.question: What vow did Hannah make if God gave her a son?

options.D: She would dedicate him to the LORD all the days of his life

questions.explanation: Hannah vowed to dedicate her son to the LORD.

questions.question: What name did Hannah give her son?
questions.explanation: She named her son Samuel.

questions.question: Where did Hannah bring Samuel to serve the LORD?
questions.explanation: She brought Samuel to the house of the LORD at Shiloh.

questions.question: How did Hannah describe her son Samuel after dedicating him?
questions.explanation: She said she had lent him to the LORD all his life.

questions.question: What did Hannah's heart do in the LORD?
questions.explanation: Hannah said her heart rejoiced in the LORD.

questions.question: What role did Samuel minister in as a child?

options.D: Before the LORD in the temple

questions.explanation: Samuel ministered before the LORD at Shiloh.

questions.question: How does the text describe Eli’s sons?
questions.explanation: They were called sons of Belial because of their wicked conduct.

questions.question: What garment did Samuel wear?
questions.explanation: He ministered wearing a linen ephod.

questions.question: What rare thing occurred in the days when Samuel was a child?

options.B: No open vision of the LORD

questions.explanation: The word of the LORD was precious and there was no open vision.

questions.question: Who did Samuel first think had called him?
questions.explanation: He first thought Eli had called him.

questions.question: What did Samuel say when the LORD called him?
questions.explanation: Samuel responded, 'Speak; for thy servant heareth.'

questions.question: What happened to Samuel as he grew?

options.C: The LORD appeared to him and was known to all Israel

questions.explanation: He grew and the LORD was with him, and all Israel knew him was established as a prophet.

questions.question: What did the elders ask Samuel to do before battle with the Philistines?
questions.explanation: They asked him to entreat the LORD for them.

questions.question: What was carried from Shiloh to the battlefield?
questions.explanation: The ark of the covenant was brought to encourage Israel.

questions.question: What happened to Eli when he heard the ark was taken?
questions.explanation: He fell backward from his seat and died when he heard the news.

questions.question: What happened to Dagon after the ark was placed in his temple?

options.D: It fell on its face before the ark

questions.explanation: Dagon fell on his face before the ark of the LORD.

questions.question: What did the Philistines place in the cart with the ark to return it?

options.C: Five golden hemorrhoids and five golden mice

questions.explanation: They used five golden hemorrhoids and five golden mice as a guilt offering.

questions.question: What did Samuel tell Israel to do in order to be delivered from the Philistines?

options.A: Put away the foreign gods and serve the LORD only

questions.explanation: He told them to put away their foreign gods and serve the LORD only.

questions.question: What did the Israelites ask Samuel to appoint for them?

options.B: A king to judge them like all nations

questions.explanation: They asked for a king to judge them like other nations.

questions.question: According to Samuel, what would a king do to their sons?

options.A: Take them for his chariots and horsemen

questions.explanation: Samuel warned a king would take sons for his chariots and horsemen.

questions.question: What was Saul’s tribe?
questions.explanation: Saul was of the tribe of Benjamin.

questions.question: What had the LORD told Samuel the day before Saul came?

options.A: Tomorrow I will send thee a man out of the land of Benjamin

questions.explanation: God told Samuel He would send a man from Benjamin the next day.

questions.question: What did Samuel pour on Saul’s head when anointing him?
questions.explanation: Samuel anointed Saul with oil from a horn.

questions.question: What happened to Saul when he left Samuel?

options.B: The Spirit of the LORD came upon him and he prophesied

questions.explanation: The Spirit of the LORD came upon Saul and he prophesied.

questions.question: Who stirred up the spirit of Israel to fight the Ammonites?

options.A: The Spirit of God upon Saul

questions.explanation: The Spirit of God came upon Saul and he was very angry.

questions.question: What did Samuel say the LORD had sought for himself?

options.C: A man after his own heart

questions.explanation: Samuel said the LORD had sought a man after his own heart.

questions.question: Who said, 'There is a lot yet among the people that have not tasted either good or bad' before battle?
questions.explanation: Jonathan said those who remain may be slain before tasting good or bad.

questions.question: Who did Saul spare when he defeated the Amalekites?

options.C: Agag and the best of the sheep and oxen

questions.explanation: He spared King Agag and the best livestock.

questions.question: What did the LORD tell Samuel about the man He had chosen to be king?

options.B: He is the one I have chosen

questions.explanation: God said He had chosen a man to be king after His own heart.

questions.question: Who anointed David with oil?
questions.explanation: Samuel anointed David as the LORD instructed.

questions.question: How tall was Goliath?
questions.explanation: Goliath was six cubits and a span tall.

questions.question: What did David say would happen to the Philistine if he fought him?

options.C: The LORD will deliver him into David’s hand

questions.explanation: David trusted the LORD to deliver Goliath into his hand.

questions.question: Why did David say the Philistine came?

options.A: To defy the armies of the living God

questions.explanation: David said Goliath came to defy the armies of the living God.

questions.question: How did David kill Goliath?

options.B: With a sling and a stone

questions.explanation: David killed Goliath with a sling and a stone.

questions.question: Who became very intimate with David after Goliath’s victory?
questions.explanation: Jonathan loved David as his own soul.

questions.question: What did Jonathan give David as a sign of his friendship?

options.C: His robe, armor, sword, bow, and belt

questions.explanation: Jonathan gave David his robe, armor, sword, bow, and belt.

questions.question: Who warned David that Saul wanted to kill him?
questions.explanation: Jonathan warned David of Saul’s intent to kill him.

questions.question: How did Michal help David escape from Saul?

options.A: She let him down through a window and lied to Saul
options.B: She told Saul to hunt elsewhere
options.D: She hid him in the temple

questions.explanation: Michal lowered David through a window and deceived Saul.

questions.question: What did David and Jonathan renew between them?

options.B: A covenant of love and loyalty

questions.explanation: They renewed a covenant of love and loyalty.

questions.question: Where did David go first when he fled from Saul?
questions.explanation: He went to Nob and met Ahimelech the priest.

questions.question: What did David do when he was afraid in Gath?
questions.explanation: He pretended madness before Achish to avoid harm.

questions.question: What did David ask the LORD before going to rescue Keilah?

options.A: If he should return to Bethlehem
options.B: If he should fight the Philistines
options.C: If the people of Keilah would deliver him into Saul’s hands

questions.explanation: David inquired if Keilah would surrender him to Saul.

questions.question: Where did David cut off part of Saul’s robe?
questions.explanation: David cut off a piece of Saul’s robe while Saul was in a cave at Engedi.

questions.question: What did David say he would not do to Saul?

options.B: Stretch forth his hand against the LORD’s anointed

questions.explanation: He refused to harm the LORD’s anointed king.

questions.question: What was Abigail’s husband Nabal described as?

options.A: Churlish and evil in his doings

questions.explanation: Nabal was described as churlish and evil in his doings.

questions.question: Who did David secretly take from Saul’s camp?

options.D: Saul’s spear and cruse of water

questions.explanation: He took Saul’s spear and a cruse of water while Saul slept.

questions.question: Why did Hannah pray in bitterness of soul?

options.B: She was deeply distressed because she could not have children

questions.explanation: Her bitterness came from barrenness and mistreatment by Peninnah.

questions.question: What did Eli’s blessing mean for Hannah after he realized she was praying?
questions.explanation: Eli blessed her that God would grant her petition for a child.

questions.question: What is the warning in Hannah’s song against arrogance?

options.C: God weighs the actions of the proud and brings them low

questions.explanation: Hannah praises God for opposing the proud and exalting the humble.

questions.question: What effect did the behavior of Eli’s sons have on Israel’s worship?

options.B: It corrupted the offerings and discredited priestly service

questions.explanation: Their wickedness harmed the integrity of the priesthood and worship.

questions.question: Why was Samuel’s calling significant in the history of Israel?

options.A: It marked the start of prophetic leadership after a time of spiritual darkness

questions.explanation: His call signaled God’s renewed active guidance through a prophet.

questions.question: What does the death of Eli reveal about God’s judgment?

options.A: God’s judgment came on the house of Eli after the ark was captured
options.B: God favored Eli to escape death
options.C: Eli died of natural causes unrelated to the ark
options.D: Eli died because of his youth

questions.explanation: Eli’s death came as a consequence of the defeat and the capture of the ark.

questions.question: Why did the people of Ashdod place the ark in Dagon’s temple?

options.C: To honor their god and show who was stronger

questions.explanation: They hoped their god Dagon would prevail over Israel’s ark.

questions.question: What does the Lord’s punishment on the Philistines show about sacred objects?

options.A: The ark is holy and not to be treated as a talisman
options.C: The ark is powerless outside Israel

questions.explanation: The ark’s capture led to plagues because it was sacred and not to be used superstitiously.

questions.question: What is the significance of Israel confessing their sins before requesting victory?

options.B: It shows they recognized their need for repentance and God’s help
options.D: It was an old ritual with no meaning

questions.explanation: Their repentance prepared them to receive deliverance from the LORD.

questions.question: Why did God tell Samuel to heed the people even though they had rejected Him?

options.A: Because they were rejecting God as their king and choosing man instead
options.B: Because God had changed His mind
options.C: Because they were right to ask for a king

questions.explanation: The request for a human king represented rejection of God’s direct rule.

questions.question: Why did God prepare Saul as the one to anoint?

options.C: Because God chose him despite future failings
options.D: Because he was the richest man

questions.explanation: God chose Saul according to his own sovereign wisdom.

questions.question: What does the Spirit of the LORD coming upon Saul indicate?

options.B: God empowered him for his immediate task and changed his behavior

questions.explanation: God’s Spirit enabled Saul for the moment and signaled divine approval.

questions.question: How did Saul bring courage to the people before attacking the Ammonites?

options.A: He divided the people and put them in positions of confidence
options.B: He gave a speech in the temple

questions.explanation: He arranged them to show strength and encouraged them to fight.

questions.question: Why was Saul’s unauthorized sacrifice a problem?

options.C: Because Saul acted contrary to God’s command and lacked patience for Samuel

questions.explanation: Saul disobeyed God’s timing by offering the sacrifice himself.

questions.question: What did Jonathan’s words about tasting good or bad reveal about his faith?

options.A: He trusted God and believed in victory through faith
options.B: He wanted to scare his men
options.D: He did not care about the battle

questions.explanation: Jonathan showed faith that if God aided them, they would survive and enjoy the results.

questions.question: Why did God regret making Saul king?

options.A: Because Saul became a better king than expected
options.B: Because Saul disobeyed and did not carry out God’s commands
options.C: Because the people asked for a king

questions.explanation: God regretted choosing Saul because of his disobedience.

questions.question: What does God value most when choosing a king?
questions.explanation: God looks at the heart rather than outward appearance.

questions.question: What was the consequence of the Spirit of the LORD departing from Saul?
questions.explanation: An evil spirit from the LORD troubled Saul after the Spirit departed.

questions.question: How did David explain his courage against Goliath?

options.C: The LORD delivered him from the lion and the bear and would deliver Goliath also

questions.explanation: David trusted God because He had delivered him before.

questions.question: Why did Saul fear David after Goliath?

options.A: Because David had success and the people praised him more

questions.explanation: Saul feared David because the people praised David’s victories and liked him.

questions.question: What happened when the Spirit of God came upon Saul while he pursued David?

options.B: He prophesied and lay naked all day and night

questions.explanation: Saul prophesied and lay naked, restrained by the Spirit of God.

questions.question: Why did David pretend to be insane in Gath?
questions.explanation: He feigned madness to escape danger from Achish.

questions.question: Why did David consult the LORD before going to Keilah?

options.A: To know whether the people of Keilah would surrender him to Saul
options.B: To ask if he should marry Abigail

questions.explanation: He sought God’s guidance about Keilah’s loyalty.

questions.question: What reason did David give for not killing Saul in the cave?

options.C: He would not stretch forth his hand against the LORD’s anointed

questions.explanation: David respected Saul as the LORD’s anointed king.

questions.question: What quality did Abigail show when she interceded with David?
questions.explanation: She acted wisely and humbly to prevent bloodshed.

questions.question: How did David compare Saul’s pursuit of him?

options.C: To an evil thing against the LORD’s anointed

questions.explanation: David said Saul’s pursuit was an evil thing against the LORD’s anointed.

questions.question: Why did David decide to stay with Achish in Gath?

options.A: He feared Saul and sought safety
options.B: He wanted to become a Philistine ruler
options.D: He was ordered by the prophet

questions.explanation: He sought refuge from Saul among the Philistines.

questions.question: Why did Saul visit the witch of Endor?

options.D: To inquire of Samuel because God stopped answering him

questions.explanation: Saul sought a medium because he received no answer from God.

questions.question: How did David behave when he learned Amalekites had destroyed Ziklag?

options.B: He encouraged himself in the LORD his God

questions.explanation: David encouraged himself in the LORD despite the disaster.

questions.question: What choice did Saul make when wounded in battle?
questions.explanation: Saul fell on his sword to avoid capture by the Philistines.

questions.question: What does Hannah’s decision to wait until Samuel was weaned before presenting him to the LORD reveal about her faith?

options.C: She trusted God’s timing and honored her vow before offering him fully

questions.explanation: She trusted God’s timing and honored her vow by waiting until he was weaned.

questions.question: How does Hannah’s song describe God’s control over life and death?

options.A: He makes poor and rich, brings down and lifts up

questions.explanation: Hannah praises God for sovereignly raising the poor and humbling the proud.

questions.question: Why is Samuel’s established prophetic authority important for Israel’s future?

options.A: It caused Eli to lose power
options.B: It prepared Israel for the transition from judges to monarchy under God’s guidance

questions.explanation: Samuel’s role as prophet helped guide Israel through its political transition.

questions.question: What mistake did Israel make when bringing the ark to battle?

options.A: They treated it as a talisman rather than honoring God’s holiness
options.B: They did not build a temple first
options.D: They hid it from the Philistines

questions.explanation: They assumed the ark itself would save them instead of relying on God’s command.

questions.question: What does Dagon falling before the ark demonstrate about idols?

options.B: They are equal to the ark
options.C: They are powerless before the true God
options.D: They were stronger than Israel’s God

questions.explanation: The event shows idols cannot stand in presence of God’s holiness.

questions.question: Why did the Philistines choose a new cart and five golden items to return the ark?

options.A: Because they wanted to keep it
options.C: Because they were ignorant of its power
options.D: Because they sought to appease Israel’s God and show repentance

questions.explanation: They offered a guilt offering to acknowledge their offense against Israel’s God.

questions.question: Why was Israel’s repentance at Mizpah essential before their victory over the Philistines?

options.A: It restored their relationship with God and opened the way for deliverance

questions.explanation: Repentance enabled God to act on their behalf.

questions.question: What does the people’s desire for a king indicate about their view of God’s rule?

options.B: They preferred human leadership to divine governance

questions.explanation: They rejected God’s direct rule in favor of an earthly king like other nations.

questions.question: How does Saul’s appearance and tribe influence Israel’s choice of him as king?

options.A: He was disqualified by his tribe
options.B: His tribe Benjamin and stature made him acceptable to some despite God choosing him for other reasons
options.D: He was chosen only by popularity

questions.explanation: Saul’s tribe and physical stature made him a plausible choice for the people, but God ultimately chose him.

questions.question: What is the meaning of Saul prophesying among the prophets?

options.A: It showed God’s Spirit empowered him and signaled divine approval at that moment
options.B: It made him become a permanent prophet
options.C: It made him unfit for kingship
options.D: It was a sign of rebellion

questions.explanation: The episode indicated God’s Spirit was upon Saul for his calling.

questions.question: How does the public confirmation of Saul as king after rescuing Jabesh-gilead reflect leadership legitimacy?

options.A: It shows victory in battle reinforced his acceptance as king
options.B: It proves the elders selected him only for peace
options.D: It was unrelated to his kingship

questions.explanation: Military success gave Saul legitimacy in the eyes of the people.

questions.question: Why did Samuel say Saul had done foolishly in offering the burnt offering?

options.C: Because Saul violated God’s command and acted without Samuel’s authority

questions.explanation: Saul disobeyed God’s timing and took priestly authority upon himself.

questions.question: What does Saul’s reaction to the people sparing the Philistines after victory reveal about his leadership?

options.B: He became angry and blamed them, revealing insecurity and poor judgment

questions.explanation: His anger showed impulsiveness and inability to lead graciously.

questions.question: What does God’s regret over Saul signify about divine-human relationships?

options.D: God is grieved by disobedience and its consequences

questions.explanation: God’s regret expresses sorrow when His chosen leader disobeys.

questions.question: What lesson does Samuel teach about obedience compared to sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Samuel said obedience is better than sacrifice.

questions.question: How does the choice of David over his older brothers challenge human expectations?

options.A: It confirms the tallest are always chosen
options.B: It shows God values the heart over outward appearance
options.C: It proves only the eldest can be chosen
options.D: It means family birth order is irrelevant

questions.explanation: God chose David for his heart rather than his looks or birth order.

questions.question: Why did an evil spirit trouble Saul after the Spirit of the LORD left him?

options.A: Because he was no longer under God’s protective influence
options.B: Because he had become physically ill

questions.explanation: The departure of God’s Spirit allowed torment by an evil spirit.

questions.question: What theological claim does David make in confronting Goliath?

options.D: The LORD of hosts will deliver Goliath into his hand

questions.explanation: David credited his victory to the LORD’s power.

questions.question: How does Saul’s jealousy of David set the stage for the rest of Saul’s reign?

options.B: It begins a downward spiral of insecurity and violence
options.C: It leads to peace with David

questions.explanation: Jealousy leads Saul into destructive behavior against David.

questions.question: What does Saul’s repeated attempt to kill David reveal about his condition?

options.C: He is consumed by fear, jealousy, and spiritual torment

questions.explanation: Saul’s attempts reflect his emotional and spiritual crisis.

questions.question: Why does David refuse to kill Saul even though he had the chance?

options.A: He respected Saul as the LORD’s anointed and trusted God to judge him
options.D: He wanted to remain in hiding

questions.explanation: He would not harm the LORD’s anointed king despite Saul’s hostility.

questions.question: What does Abigail’s intervention teach about conflict resolution?

options.D: Wise humility and timely action can avert bloodshed

questions.explanation: Abigail’s wisdom and humility prevented David from bloodguilt.

questions.question: How does David’s respect for Saul in giving him his spear and cruse of water illustrate his character?

options.B: He remained honorable and compassionate toward his enemy

questions.explanation: David showed honor and restraint toward Saul despite being hunted.

questions.question: What does David’s choice to live among the Philistines reveal about his desperation?

options.A: He was willing to risk enemy territory for safety from Saul
options.C: He sought royal power in Gath

questions.explanation: He chose refuge among enemies due to fear of Saul.

questions.question: What is the spiritual significance of Samuel’s appearance to Saul?

options.B: It shows the tragedy of Saul’s separation from God and the certainty of his fate
options.C: It indicates God endorsed Saul’s actions

questions.explanation: The encounter underscores Saul’s spiritual ruin and the inevitability of God’s judgment.

questions.question: Why is David’s encouragement of himself in the LORD important?

options.A: It demonstrates his reliance on God rather than despair in crisis
options.B: It shows he had no faith
options.D: It was a sign of weakness

questions.explanation: He drew strength from God when faced with disaster.

questions.question: What does Saul’s suicide communicate about his state of mind at the end?

options.A: He had won a great victory
options.C: He was desperate and chose death over capture

questions.explanation: Saul chose death to avoid humiliation and capture.

questions.question: What does Samuel’s warning to fear and serve the LORD teach about kingship?

options.A: Kings should rely on their military power
options.D: True leadership requires fear of God and obedience

questions.explanation: Samuel stressed that obedience to God is essential for good leadership.

questions.question: What was wrong with Saul’s oath after the battle with the Philistines?

options.B: It forbade eating during the pursuit, harming his own troops and Jonathan
options.D: It was made to the wrong god

questions.explanation: The oath was impractical and caused unnecessary hardship.

questions.question: How does Samuel equate rebellion with witchcraft and stubbornness with idolatry?

options.A: He underscores that disobedience to God is as serious as spiritual rebellion

questions.explanation: Samuel teaches that obedience matters more than religious ritual.

questions.question: What contrast is posed between Saul and David regarding the Spirit of the LORD?

options.A: David is empowered by God while Saul is rejected and tormented

questions.explanation: David’s life is marked by God’s blessing while Saul experiences God’s departure.

questions.question: Why does David’s confidence in God contrast with Israel’s fear of Goliath?

options.B: Because David trusted in God’s deliverance rather than human strength

questions.explanation: David’s faith stood against the army’s fear of the giant.

questions.question: How does Jonathan’s covenant with David model friendship?

options.A: It shows loyalty, love, and mutual support despite external pressures
options.B: It was a political alliance only

questions.explanation: Their covenant demonstrates deep friendship beyond convenience.

questions.question: What does Saul prophesying among prophets reveal about his relationship with God?

options.B: He had become a prophet instead of a king
options.D: God’s Spirit still acted in him unpredictably despite his failure

questions.explanation: It shows God’s Spirit still worked in Saul even as he disobeyed.

questions.question: Why is David’s madness before Achish both shrewd and risky?

options.A: It preserved his life but risked offending a powerful pagan king

questions.explanation: David used deception to avoid danger while staying among enemies.

questions.question: What does the presence of the LORD with David in the wilderness indicate?

options.B: God continued to be with him despite Saul’s pursuit

questions.explanation: David remained under God’s care during his flight from Saul.

questions.question: What does David’s restraint in the cave teach about justice and mercy?

options.A: He preferred mercy and left judgment to God

questions.explanation: He chose mercy and trusted God to judge Saul.

questions.question: Why does David praise Abigail after her intervention?

options.C: Because she prevented him from bloodguilt and showed wisdom

questions.explanation: He recognized her role in averting sin and conflict.

questions.question: What does David giving Saul his spear and water suggest about his trust?

options.B: He trusted Saul would see his innocence and God’s righteousness
options.C: He wanted Saul to kill him

questions.explanation: He showed confidence that Saul would recognize God’s will.

questions.question: How does the prophecy of Samuel to Saul affect Saul’s final days?

options.C: It encourages him to fight bravely
options.D: It predicts his defeat and death, sealing his tragic end

questions.explanation: Samuel foretold Israel’s defeat and Saul’s death, confirming his doom.

questions.question: What does David’s recovery of all that was taken from Ziklag show about his leadership?

options.A: He was determined, strategic, and faithful when under stress

questions.explanation: He pursued and recovered everything, showing strong leadership.

questions.question: Why did the men of Jabesh-gilead risk rescuing Saul’s body?

options.C: To honor him and prevent abuse by the Philistines

questions.explanation: They honored Saul by rescuing and burying his body properly.

questions.question: What does Samuel mean by God being your salvation if you fear the LORD and serve him?

options.A: God will save you from all physical danger
options.B: Obedience to God brings spiritual preservation and blessing

questions.explanation: He connects true salvation with fearing and serving God.

questions.question: How does Saul’s anger at the people after they spared the Philistines contrast with divine mercy?

options.A: He reacted harshly while God’s mercy would have preserved life

questions.explanation: Saul’s harsh judgment clashes with God’s call to mercy and justice.

questions.question: What does Samuel’s refusal to see Saul again signify?

options.B: Saul was rejected by God and the prophetic relationship ended

questions.explanation: It marked the end of Saul’s standing before the prophet and God.

questions.question: Why is the contrast between David anointed and Saul tormented important for the book’s theme?

options.A: It highlights the shift from Saul’s failed kingship to David’s emerging hope supported by God
options.B: It shows that kingship is always cursed
options.C: It proves all physical traits matter
options.D: It emphasizes military power over spiritual condition

questions.explanation: The book contrasts Saul’s loss of God’s favor with David’s rise under God’s blessing.

questions.question: What larger truth does David assert about battle and the LORD?

options.A: The battle belongs to the LORD and He gives victory to those who trust Him

questions.explanation: David attributes victory to the LORD, not merely human power.

questions.question: How does Jonathan giving David his robe and weapons symbolize the transfer of favor?

options.B: It showed his willingness to share royal privilege and support David’s future
options.C: It was a gift of war booty
options.D: It was a sign of surrender

questions.explanation: The gifts symbolized Jonathan’s love and recognition of David’s destiny.

questions.question: What does Michal’s deception reveal about her loyalty?

options.A: She was loyal to Saul only
options.C: She risked her position to save David from Saul

questions.explanation: She protected David despite her father’s hatred of him.

questions.question: What does David’s decision to claim he was on a secret mission reveal about the complexities of survival?

options.A: He was willing to use deception to stay alive under threat
options.B: He had no sense of danger

questions.explanation: He used cunning to protect himself in dangerous territory.

questions.question: What does Saul’s earnest pursuit of David after Keilah suggest about his motives?

options.A: He wanted to make David king
options.D: He was determined to eliminate David as a rival

questions.explanation: Saul pursued David relentlessly because he saw him as a threat.

questions.question: How does David’s speech to Saul in the cave appeal to Saul’s conscience?

options.A: By reminding him of his anointed status and accusing him of wrongdoing

questions.explanation: David called Saul out for unjustly pursuing the LORD’s anointed one.

questions.question: What does Abigail’s obedience to Nabal show about her character?

options.B: She took responsibility to prevent disaster despite her husband’s folly

questions.explanation: She acted to save lives even though Nabal was foolish.

questions.question: Why does David say Saul has filled the land with murderers?

options.A: To show the evil of Saul’s pursuit of his own servant
options.D: To brag about his own power

questions.explanation: David condemns Saul’s unjust violent campaign against him.

questions.question: What is ironic about David dwelling with Achish?

options.C: He sought safety in the land of his people’s enemy

questions.explanation: He took refuge among enemies to escape an Israelite king.

questions.question: Why did the revelation of complete defeat through Samuel impact Saul’s leadership?

options.A: It confirmed his tragic end and the failure of his kingship
options.B: It encouraged him to fight harder

questions.explanation: It marked the final collapse of Saul’s reign and hope.

questions.question: What does David’s recovery of all the spoil from the Amalekites say about his covenant with his men?

options.B: He kept his promise to share the reward and restore all that was lost

questions.explanation: He honored his agreement and restored the spoils to his men.

questions.question: What does the respectful burial of Saul and his sons by Jabesh-gilead reveal?

options.B: They wanted the Philistines to loot them
options.C: They honored Saul despite his tragic end because he was Israel’s king

questions.explanation: They honored their king with burial despite political risk.

questions.question: Why is Saul confirmed as king at Gilgal after victory important?

options.A: It demonstrates that military success secured popular acceptance of his rule
options.B: It shows he had no support
options.C: It shows he was only a temporary leader
options.D: It means he was spiritually qualified

questions.explanation: His kingship was solidified by victory and public acclamation.

questions.question: What does Samuel’s mourning for Saul indicate about God’s mercy?

options.B: God lamented the loss of a king who might have been faithful
options.D: God immediately chose another without regret

questions.explanation: Samuel’s mourning shows God’s sorrow over Saul’s wasted potential.

questions.question: How does David’s anointing foreshadow the future of Israel?

options.A: It sets the stage for the Davidic dynasty and God’s promise of a king after His own heart
options.B: It proves the line of Saul would continue forever
options.D: It means David would never rule

questions.explanation: David’s anointing points toward the lasting Davidic covenant.

questions.question: Why does David’s victory over Goliath matter beyond a single battle?

options.B: It displayed God’s power through an unlikely servant and changed Israel’s destiny

questions.explanation: It marked the rise of David and God’s favor on him.

questions.question: What does Jonathan’s gift of royal armor to David symbolize in light of Saul’s jealousy?

options.C: Jonathan’s personal support and recognition of David’s future
options.D: A military alliance with the Philistines

questions.explanation: The gift signifies Jonathan’s love and loyalty toward David.

questions.question: What paradox is present when Saul prophesies while seeking David’s life?

options.B: David was cursed by the Spirit
options.D: The Spirit of God empowered Saul even as he acted against God’s anointed

questions.explanation: God’s Spirit worked in Saul despite his hostile actions toward David.

questions.question: What does David’s ability to deceive Achish suggest about his survival strategy?

options.B: He used cunning when necessary to preserve life
options.C: He trusted Saul to protect him

questions.explanation: He balanced faith with practical wisdom to survive.

questions.question: How does God answering David about Keilah demonstrate divine care?

options.A: It shows God knows and guides even in small local matters

questions.explanation: God’s answer affirmed His involvement in David’s immediate situation.

questions.question: What does David’s tearing of Saul’s skirt instead of killing him symbolize?

options.B: He wished to mock the king
options.D: He spared Saul while proving he could have killed him

questions.explanation: It showed restraint and proof of mercy toward Saul.

questions.question: What does Abigail’s quick action in bringing gifts to David reveal about her priorities?

options.A: She valued peace and protection over her husband’s pride

questions.explanation: She prioritized the safety and righteousness of her household.

questions.question: How does David’s release of Saul affirm his trust in God’s sovereignty over kingship?

options.A: It means he wanted to usurp the throne
options.B: It shows he feared Saul more than God
options.C: It shows he believed God would establish the right king in His time

questions.explanation: David deferred judgment to God and trusted God’s timing for kingship.

questions.question: Why does the rescue and burial of Saul by Jabesh-gilead matter in the narrative?

options.A: It proves Saul was still king
options.B: It honors Israel’s king and preserves dignity despite his tragic end
options.C: It shows they feared the Philistines

questions.explanation: Their act preserved Saul’s dignity and respect for Israel’s monarchy.
